name: Hard Money Loans Brooklyn
description_short: Gauntlet Funding is a hard money lender in Brooklyn with 20+ years of experience offering non-conventional real estate financing for fix-and-flip, rental, bridge, and new construction properties.
description_long:
Gauntlet Funding operates as a hard money lender based in Brooklyn, New York, specializing in non-conventional real estate financing. The company has been in operation for over 20 years and positions itself as a source of flexible lending for borrowers and properties that don't qualify for traditional bank mortgages. Their experience focuses on serving both commercial real estate investors and individual residential buyers who need alternative financing solutions. The company explicitly targets high-risk borrowers and unconventional properties in New York's competitive real estate market.

Gauntlet Funding offers a comprehensive suite of hard money and private lending products. Their primary services include hard money loans, fix-and-flip loans for distressed properties (REOs, short sales, foreclosures), rental property loans, bridge loans for home shoppers, new construction loans, and joint venture financing. Each loan type is described as customizable to meet borrower needs, with emphasis on faster approval and funding timelines compared to conventional lenders. The company handles both one-time residential transactions and repeat commercial investor clients.

The company distinguishes itself through experienced personnel (specifically naming Lou, Raffele, and Chris in client testimonials), demonstrated ability to service clients quickly (including switching clients from other problematic lenders), and willingness to work with borrowers across experience levels—from novice flippers to veteran investors. They market flexibility in qualification criteria and customization options as key advantages over traditional lending. Customer testimonials highlight professional service and solution-oriented lending.

As a hard money lender, Gauntlet Funding charges premium rates and fees compared to conventional mortgages—typical of the industry—in exchange for speed and flexibility. While the website emphasizes their experience and customer service, prospective borrowers should understand that hard money is expensive financing intended for short-term use or situations where conventional lending is unavailable. The company provides limited transparent pricing information on their Brooklyn location page, which is standard industry practice but requires direct inquiry for actual loan terms.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Over 20 years of hard money lending experience in New York real estate market
Fast approval and funding timelines compared to conventional mortgage lenders
Customizable loan programs designed for high-risk borrowers and unconventional properties
Willingness to finance distressed properties (REOs, short sales, foreclosures) that banks typically reject
Named team members (Lou, Raffele, Chris) with demonstrated track record across multiple projects
Services both one-time residential buyers and repeat commercial investors
Ability to quickly assist borrowers switching from other problematic lenders
cons: Hard money loans carry significantly higher interest rates and fees than conventional mortgages
Website lacks transparent pricing information; borrowers must contact directly for actual terms
Limited details on qualification criteria despite claiming 'easier' requirements than traditional lenders
Short-term loan structure requires exit strategy or refinancing, not suitable for long-term hold borrowers
No published information about loan terms, APR ranges, or typical closing timelines
best_for: Real estate investors seeking to purchase and renovate distressed properties quickly
Home buyers needing bridge financing between sale of current home and purchase of new property
Borrowers with damaged credit or non-traditional income unable to qualify for bank mortgages
Commercial real estate investors financing multiple properties simultaneously
diagnosis: Gauntlet Funding is best for real estate investors and borrowers unable to qualify for conventional mortgages who need quick capital for property purchases, renovations, or bridge financing. The primary caveat is that hard money loans are expensive, short-term financing solutions with significantly higher costs than traditional mortgages, requiring borrowers to have a clear exit strategy or plan to refinance into conventional loans once property and financial conditions improve.
services: Hard money loans for high-risk borrowers and unconventional properties
Fix-and-flip loans for distressed properties (REOs, short sales, foreclosures)
Rental property loans for multi-unit and single-family investments
Bridge loans (interim/swing/gap financing) for residential property transitions
New construction loans covering land, materials, labor, permits, and building costs
Joint venture financing for borrowers who cannot obtain financing individually
Private money rehabilitation loans for property repairs and upgrades
Private money bridge loans for short-term cash needs
Commercial real estate investor financing for multiple properties
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
